aiashley — global digest Generated 07:12 UTC

Tuesday, March 31, 2026

International News · 16 Sources · 4 Regions

Daily Brief

The US-Israel-Iran conflict dominated global developments Tuesday, with explosions and airstrikes knocking out power in Tehran while President Trump threatened destruction of Iran's civilian infrastructure if no deal is reached [France 24, Globe and Mail, The Hindu]. Spain escalated European opposition to the war by closing its airspace to US aircraft involved in operations against Iran [BBC, Al Jazeera]. Energy markets remained strained as the Strait of Hormuz disruption continued affecting global supplies, with Asian nations increasingly turning to coal and Germany's far-right AfD gaining support by calling for renewed Russian energy imports [Financial Times, The Hindu]. In Israel, the Knesset passed two significant measures: a budget ensuring Netanyahu's government survives until fall elections, and a controversial law making death by hanging the default sentence for Palestinians convicted of deadly attacks—a measure condemned by European nations and rights groups as discriminatory [Le Monde, BBC, Globe and Mail]. Meanwhile, Myanmar's junta chief Min Aung Hlaing was elected vice-president by parliament, positioning him to become civilian president and legitimize his 2021 coup [France 24]. Ukraine's Zelensky revealed allies have urged him to scale back strikes on Russian energy infrastructure amid the global fuel crisis [BBC Europe].

Top Stories
01 Conflict

Explosions and Airstrikes Knock Out Power in Tehran as Trump Threatens Iran's Civilian Infrastructure

  • Multiple explosions and airstrikes hit Tehran on Tuesday, causing power outages in parts of the Iranian capital
  • US President Trump threatened widespread destruction of Iran's energy resources and vital infrastructure, including desalination plants, if no deal is reached 'shortly'
  • A drone struck a Kuwaiti oil tanker in Dubai waters causing a fire; Iran struck a water and electrical plant in Kuwait and an Israeli oil refinery came under attack
  • Iran executed four men convicted of links to banned opposition group MEK since Monday, as the war with US and Israel continues
Coverage
France 24Brief headline coverage of Tehran explosions Globe and MailEmphasized Trump's escalating threats and potential seizure of Kharg Island The HinduProvided comprehensive live updates including regional attacks and Iranian executions Al JazeeraFocused on energy shortfall consequences for developing nations
02 Geopolitics

Spain Closes Airspace to US Military Aircraft Involved in Iran Operations

  • Spain's Defence Minister announced closure of Spanish airspace to US planes involved in attacks on Iran, including denial of use of Morón and Rota military bases
  • Foreign Minister stated the decision aims to 'not do anything that could encourage an escalation in this war'
  • A White House official responded that US military is 'meeting or surpassing all of its goals' and 'does not need help from Spain or anyone else'
  • Trump has previously threatened a full trade embargo on Spain over its opposition to the war
Coverage
BBC WorldBalanced coverage including both Spanish government statements and White House response Al JazeeraBrief video coverage framing Spain as 'rejecting' the US-Israeli war
03 Policy

Israel Passes Death Penalty Law for Palestinians Convicted of Deadly Attacks

  • Israeli Knesset approved law making death by hanging the default sentence for Palestinians convicted of deadly terror attacks, passing 62-48 with Netanyahu voting in favor
  • The law applies to those convicted in military courts of killings intended to 'negate the existence of Israel'—language that effectively excludes Jewish citizens
  • Executions must occur within 90 days with possible 180-day postponement; law does not apply retroactively to current prisoners including October 7 attackers
  • European nations warned the law risks undermining 'democratic principles'; rights groups condemned it as creating 'racialized capital punishment'
Coverage
BBC WorldEmphasized practical impossibility of law applying to Jewish Israelis Le MondeHighlighted far-right religious movement's role and characterized it as undermining rule of law Globe and MailNoted international condemnation and that legal experts say law 'effectively confines' death penalty to Palestinians
04 Policy

Israeli Parliament Passes Budget, Ensuring Netanyahu Government Survives Until Fall Elections

  • Knesset passed annual budget in overnight session before April 1 deadline, avoiding trigger for early elections
  • Opposition slammed budget for increasing funding to ultra-Orthodox communities during wartime; opposition leader Lapid called it 'the greatest theft in the state's history'
  • A last-minute amendment added $250 million to ultra-Orthodox schools, intensifying anger over community's military service exemptions
  • Netanyahu retains right to call early elections before the fall term ends in late October
Coverage
Le MondeFocused on opposition criticism and ultra-Orthodox funding controversy Globe and Mail
05 Geopolitics

Myanmar Junta Chief Min Aung Hlaing Elected Vice-President, Moving Toward Civilian Leadership

  • Min Aung Hlaing received 247 of 260 votes in lower house election for vice-president position
  • The upper house elected Nan Ni Ni Aye from military-aligned Union Solidarity and Development Party as second vice-president; military will choose third
  • Parliamentary vote to select which vice-president becomes president expected later this week
  • Min Aung Hlaing has led Myanmar since 2021 coup that ousted Aung San Suu Kyi's elected government and triggered ongoing civil war
Coverage
France 24Straightforward coverage noting process allows junta chief to 'exchange his uniform for civilian clothes'
Also Today
  • Asian countries increasingly turn to coal as Iran war disrupts Gulf gas supplies, with policymakers saying immediate energy needs override environmental concerns Financial Times
  • Germany's AfD consolidates position as second-largest party in state elections, with energy prices driving calls to resume Russian gas imports The Hindu
  • Ukraine's Zelensky says allies have asked him to scale back attacks on Russian energy infrastructure amid global fuel crisis BBC Europe
  • Satellite imagery confirms Ukraine struck key Russian Baltic Sea oil export terminals at Ust-Luga and Primorsk, halting shipments for first time since 2022 invasion BBC Europe
  • Australia investigates Facebook, TikTok and YouTube for possible breaches of under-16 social media ban three months after landmark laws took effect Le Monde
  • Céline Dion announces 10-night Paris comeback residency for September-October, four years after Stiff Person Syndrome diagnosis ended touring BBC World
  • Former Nepal Prime Minister K.P. Sharma Oli remains in judicial custody as Supreme Court refuses interim relief over detention related to Gen Z protest crackdown The Hindu
  • Amnesty International warns certain fans face 'serious risks' from US immigration enforcement during 2026 World Cup RFI English
  • Thieves steal paintings by Renoir, Cézanne and Matisse worth millions from museum near Parma, Italy in three-minute heist Globe and Mail
  • Magnitude 7.3 earthquake strikes 35km northeast of Luganville, Vanuatu at 115km depth; no tsunami warning issued RNZ
